results.title: Gemini image generation experience was extremely frustrating. I provided a detailed professional pr
results.content:
1.   Remove any specific company names, brands, or trademarked terms from your prompt. 
2.   Replace phrases like "real production-grade" with terms like "conceptual mockup," "abstract SaaS dashboard," or "wireframe UI layout." 
3.   Clear your browser cache and cookies, or open a new Incognito window to break the current chat loop. 
4.   Click New chat to start a completely fresh session and try the modified prompt. 

Because the error handling you experienced is a flaw in how the system communicates failure states, please submit a feedback report so the engineering team can investigate the broken loop: [...] # Gemini image generation experience was extremely frustrating. I provided a detailed professional pr - Gemini Apps Community

Soham Shah 5130

The issue you are running into is likely caused by an overlap between Gemini's safety filters and a system fallback glitch: [...] Trademark Filters: When prompts include specific corporate names (like Stripe, Salesforce, SAP, IBM, Atlassian) or request "real" UI interfaces, the image generation engine often blocks the request to prevent copyright infringement or the creation of deceptive mockups. 
   System Fallback Error: When the image is blocked, the text engine sometimes incorrectly tries to fulfill the request by describing what it _would_ have generated, leading to the confusing error loop you experienced. 

To get your images generating correctly, please try these troubleshooting steps:

results.title: Frustrations with ChatGPT Image Generation - Experiencing Elearning
results.content:
# Frustrations with ChatGPT Image Generation

Like many L&D folks, I’ve been experimenting with ChatGPT image generation. Sometimes, ChatGPT does great! But other times, I find it really frustrating. I’ve been working on a scenario recently for a client project, and I need a series of images of two characters talking together. Midjourney is great for generating images of consistent characters, but only with one character at a time. So, I thought I’d try ChatGPT for this project instead. What I discovered is that ChatGPT’s consistency in characters degrades over multiple generations. I also struggled with the position of the characters; ChatGPT keeps placing characters so they aren’t looking at each other. [...] Here’s a side-by-side comparison of the graduate student character so the inconsistency is more obvious. I always expect some minor inconsistency in AI image generation; that’s part of the reality of working with these tools. But to have a character shift this much after only a handful of iterations was really disappointing. I think ChatGPT does better with character consistency when you’re only working with a single character. In a scene with even two characters, I don’t think it’s usable if the characters change so much. [...] I started by generating a single character, the grad student. So far, so good. This is a usable image.

A 25-year-old Black woman and graduate student taking notes 

Generating an image of this same character talking worked OK too. The character and scene consistency is pretty good.

The same graduate student as the previous image, but talking.

## Adding a second character

Adding a second character, the professor, is where things got trickier. I prompted ChatGPT to change the angle to a side view so we can see the other person in the conversation.

results.title: Broken Image Generation System – Killing Creative Workflows - Use cases and examples - OpenAI Developer Community
results.content:
# Broken Image Generation System – Killing Creative Workflows

Dear OpenAI Team,

I’m writing to express serious frustration with the current state of your image generation tools—specifically the recent “upgrades” that have crippled functionality for creators like me.

Here’s what I’ve experienced:

### :cross_mark: What’s Broken

:cross_mark:

Face Referencing Is Inconsistent  
Despite uploading a face reference image, the tool fails to reproduce consistent likeness—even when using the exact same prompt. This is unacceptable for character-driven projects.

Rate Limits Are Overly Harsh  
Trying to iterate on a visual—adjusting poses, muscles, or clothing—now hits cooldowns constantly. I can’t even refine a single design without being locked out for 10+ minutes. [...] ### Related topics

| Image Generation - Edits Qualify as Broken in current behavior  Feedback | 1 | 665 | April 30, 2025 |
| Very confused about the content policy  Community gpt-4 ,  api-writers ,  api-writing ,  content-policy | 9 | 14312 | June 29, 2024 |

Powered by Discourse, best viewed with JavaScript enabled [...] ### :bullseye: What I Need as a Creator

:bullseye:

If OpenAI wants to support real creators—those building deep lore, stories, and characters—you need to fix the system you broke and start empowering creators, not stifling them.

Right now, it feels like you’re tuning the platform for casual toy use, not for professionals or world-builders.

I hope this gets taken seriously, because this platform has incredible potential. But if these issues aren’t addressed soon, you’re going to lose the people who care the most.

Sincerely,  
A seriously disappointed creator

results.title: Image Generation Is Ruined – Why Did You Do This? - ChatGPT - OpenAI Developer Community
results.content:
# Image Generation Is Ruined – Why Did You Do This?

What happened to image generation? This new update is ridiculous. The results are worse, slower, and full of arbitrary limitations that make zero sense for people who create content professionally. The images are bland, less creative, and often miss the mark entirely – and don’t even get me started on the lack of control or consistency.

Wasn’t this supposed to be a pro tool? Because right now it feels like we’re being forced to work with a broken toy instead of a creative engine. For those of us who rely on high-quality, customizable visuals, this change isn’t just annoying – it’s a real problem. We’re wasting time, losing quality, and wondering: why fix what wasn’t broken? [...] Don’t ever describe a woman physically - ie height weight looks clothes. The censors freak out.

Otherwise, for general use it’s good, for creative usage, it would have been good without the filtering restricting what you can do with the AI, I wish they added a function where you can turn off the “safe mode”, cause in itself, the quality of image creation did upgrade, with much less artifacts appearing in frequency.

| Image API access: “image\_generation\_user\_error” on paid API account, but images work in Playground  Bugs image-generation | 2 | 1104 | July 9, 2025 | [...] # Image generation not working – diagnostic guide & community call

Hello everyone,

Since April 14, 2025, multiple attempts to generate images via image\_gen (integrated DALL·E module) have failed silently — no image is returned, and no error is shown, even with clean prompts.

Tested prompt: “Two AIs talking together in a futuristic technological universe”

Observed issues:

No image is returned, even when the prompt is accepted.

No visible error code in the UI or console.

Reproducible across multiple browsers and devices.

Prompts are well-formed and varied in style, yet none yield output.

Local troubleshooting guide (partial success)

Here are a few strategies I’ve tested that might help others:

Change visual style (e.g., “digital painting” vs. “realistic” vs. “line art”).
